summary: Doc-only follow-up addressing gndrmnn's review #3: removes the trailing sentence about preserving the *TrustFlushSpec pointer shape 'for backwards compatibility with envtest fixtures' from the TrustFlush godoc on KeystoneSpec. Regenerated CRD YAMLs (config/crd/bases and helm/keystone-operator/crds) pick up the same change so all three locations stay in sync. Two review-pattern files were added under .planwerk/review_patterns/ to capture the lessons: (1) avoid backwards-compatibility justifications in pre-release projects, and (2) ensure comments accurately describe the actual invariant. Progress JSON moved from awaiting_external_review back to improving. No code logic, no tests, no public API contract changes — the field shape, defaulting webhook behavior, reconciler bypass branch, and the +kubebuilder:default markers are all untouched.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (unchanged — no code logic touched in this cycle)
Edge cases are covered (no behavior change, prior coverage applies)
Docs-only change does not require new tests

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing godoc conventions on sibling fields (Federation, Autoscaling, NetworkPolicy)
CRD YAMLs are regenerated, not hand-edited — godoc remains the single source of truth
No leftover commented-out text or TODO breadcrumbs in the diff
Change scope matches gndrmnn's three review comments exactly (keystone_types.go:84 + two CRD YAMLs)

security_checklist.item: No security-sensitive code paths affected by a doc-only edit
No secrets, credentials, or auth flow touched

architecture_checklist.item: +kubebuilder:default markers on Schedule and Suspend remain in place (defense-in-depth criterion)
Solution is as simple as possible — single godoc paragraph trimmed, two regenerated YAMLs follow

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated text — godoc edited once, both CRDs regenerated from it
No new abstractions or helpers added

defensive_checklist.item: Validation paths unchanged (no input boundaries crossed)

next_steps: Push the branch and re-request review from gndrmnn so review #3 can be closed out.
